title: Mulicity and mixed cabin is it worth it?
body:
O.P.
We are looking at northern Italy next year and trying to figure out flight options. One option bring considered is to book a mulicity and mixed cabin fare as follows;
HBA to MEL/SYD to pre-position overnight stay.
MEL/SYD to SIN daytime flight in economy probably Singapore airlines or Qantas. Few days in Singapore.
SIN to FCO/MXP likely via LHR/MUC/ZRH if we cannot get direct. This will be overnight so looking at business class for long haul and economy for last leg.
FCO/MXP to BKK, also likely via MUC/ZRH/LHR, overnight so again business. Few days in BKK.
BKK to MEL/SYD aim for daytime flight in economy. Overnight then back to HBA. If this is an overnight flight we would book business and try and get a connection back to HBA.
We are not keen on a long haul overnight in economy. We've done HND to SYD and ICN to SYD in the last year and it is not fun particularly with the extra hop back to HBA.
The actual route is still loose as there are several airline combinations possible. QF and BA or Singapore and Lufthansa/Swiss.
My question is. Is it worth the hassle to book mixed cabin? Are there decent cost savings to be made? Full economy is around $2500 pp. Full business is about $10k pp.
